description: This set of figures presents bainitic transformation kinetics of low-density nano-bainitic Al-C steels (samples S1-S5 with varying Al, C and alloying element contents) measured via dilatometry, plotted as relative length change (RLC, %) versus time (s). Subfigures (a) and (b) adopt logarithmic time axes: (a) shows data for steels with Al ≥ 6 wt.% (S1, S2, S3) under different isothermal bainitic holding temperatures (270℃, 300℃, 350℃); (b) shows data for steels with Al < 6 wt.% (S4, S5) under different austenitization and isothermal holding temperatures (300℃, 335℃, 350℃, 365℃). Subfigures (c)-(e) replot the same data with linear time axes for better readability of transformation times: (c) for S1/S2, (d) for the slow-transforming S3 with extended time range, (e) for S4/S5, with vertical dashed lines marking approximate bainite finish times. Key trends show higher isothermal temperature accelerates transformation, higher Al content remarkably slows transformation (bainite finish time of S3 reaches up to 43000 s), while Ti-microalloyed S4 and low-Al S5 have much faster transformation (minimum finish time ~700 s). The figure supports that Al content, isothermal temperature and Ti microalloying significantly regulate bainitic transformation kinetics of the tested steels.
